posts.content:
Three years ago I watched a friend lose a KES 2.3 million government contract.

Not because his company was unqualified.
Not because his bid was weak.
Because he found out about the tender four days after the deadline closed.

He had done everything right. Registered on AGPO. Got his KRA compliance in order. Built a solid team. But the opportunity was listed on a portal he had never heard of and by the time someone forwarded him the link it was already gone.

That moment stuck with me.

Kenya's government spends billions of KES every year procuring goods and services. A full 30% of national procurement is legally reserved for youth, women and PWD owned businesses through AGPO. The money is there. The legal framework is there. The opportunities are real.

But the information is scattered across nine different portals. PPIP. NCC. KenGen. KeNHA. KeRRA. KURA. KPC. KAA. GDC. No single place to see everything. No alerts when a tender matches your business. No guidance on what to prepare.

So I started building TenderIQ.

Over the next 30 days I am going to share exactly what I am building and why. Follow along if you run an SME in Kenya and government procurement has ever felt like a system designed to keep you out.

It was not designed to keep you out. It just was not designed to let you in easily either. That is what we are fixing.

Join the waitlist at https://forms.gle/cCqPeLPxxoFZxY616

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #Kenya #Procurement

I want to tell you about the morning that made TenderIQ inevitable.

I was doing research for this product. Sat down at 8am and tried to manually find every active government tender in Kenya relevant to an IT company.

By 11am I had checked four portals. Each one had a different login. Different search interface. Different date formats. One portal had tenders listed with no closing dates at all. Another had a search bar that returned nothing regardless of what I typed.

I found three potentially relevant tenders. Two of them had closed the previous week. The third had a closing date of the following day.

Three hours. One usable opportunity. One day to respond.

This is what Kenyan SME owners are dealing with every single time they try to participate in government procurement. Not once. Every. Single. Time.

And that is the owner who is motivated enough to go looking. Most do not bother because the last time they tried it took half a day and led nowhere.

TenderIQ aggregates every tender across all nine major government procurement portals into one place. Matched to your business profile. Delivered before the deadline gives you no room to move.

You should be spending your time preparing a winning bid. Not hunting for one.

Waitlist is open at https://forms.gle/cCqPeLPxxoFZxY616

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #Procurement #Kenya

People ask me why a developer decided to build a procurement platform.

Honest answer: because I got angry.

I kept seeing the same pattern. Talented, compliant, capable Kenyan businesses that could not break into government procurement not because they lacked capacity but because they lacked information.

The businesses winning consistently were not always the most qualified. They were the most informed. They had someone watching the portals daily. They had connections who called them when something relevant came up. They had systems the average SME could not afford.

That is not a level playing field. That is a structural advantage being enjoyed by the few at the expense of the many.

I am a builder. When I see a structural problem I look for a systems solution.

TenderIQ is that solution. AI that watches every procurement portal so you do not have to. Matching tenders to your profile the moment they are published. Analyzing requirements so you know instantly whether you qualify. Tracking deadlines so nothing slips.

This is not complicated technology. It is focused technology solving a specific, painful, real problem for Kenyan SMEs.

If this is the kind of product you have been waiting for, the waitlist is at https://forms.gle/cCqPeLPxxoFZxY616

I read every response. This is still early.

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #Procurement #GovernmentContracts #Kenya

There are nine major government procurement portals in Kenya.

PPIP. NCC. KenGen. KeNHA. KeRRA. KURA. KPC. KAA. GDC.

How many does your business check regularly?

If the answer is one or two you are already missing the majority of tenders you qualify for. Not because you are not trying. Because nobody built a system that makes checking all nine realistic for a small business team.

Here is what monitoring all nine portals manually actually costs you:

Time: Roughly two to three hours per week if you are thorough. More if portals are slow or down.

Attention: Every hour spent hunting for tenders is an hour not spent running your business or preparing a better bid.

Missed opportunities: The tenders you never found because they were posted on a portal you did not check that week.

TenderIQ monitors all nine portals continuously. When a tender is published that matches your business profile you get notified. You do not go looking. The relevant opportunities come to you.

The businesses that consistently win government contracts are not smarter than yours. They just have better information systems.

You can have the same system.

Join the waitlist at https://forms.gle/cCqPeLPxxoFZxY616

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #PPIP #Procurement

Let me tell you what actually happens when a Kenyan SME misses a tender deadline.

It is not just a lost contract. It is a lost three to six months.

Government procurement cycles in Kenya run quarterly to annually. If you miss a tender for office supplies to a county government in January the next opportunity for the same contract may not come until mid year or later. Sometimes longer.

Each missed deadline is not a small setback. It is a quarter of potential revenue gone. A team that was ready to deliver sitting idle. A business case for growth that does not close.

And the cruel part is that most of these misses are avoidable. The tender was published. Your business qualified. The deadline passed before you ever knew it existed.

TenderIQ sends you alerts the moment a matching tender is published. Not when it is two days from closing. When it is fresh. So you have the maximum possible time to prepare a complete and competitive bid.

Deadline anxiety is a symptom of a broken information system. You should not be scrambling. You should be preparing.

Get on the waitlist at https://forms.gle/cCqPeLPxxoFZxY616

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #Procurement #Kenya

AGPO gave Kenyan SMEs access to 30% of national government procurement.

That is billions of KES reserved specifically for youth, women and PWD owned businesses every year.

So why are so many AGPO registered businesses still not winning tenders?

I have talked to dozens of SME owners about this. The answers cluster around three problems every time.

First: they do not know which tenders fall under AGPO and which do not, so they either apply for everything and waste time or apply for nothing and miss out.

Second: they find tenders too late. By the time the information reaches them through a WhatsApp forward or a colleague's tip there are two days left to prepare a compliant bid.

Third: they do not know exactly what documentation is required for each tender so they submit incomplete bids and get disqualified on technicalities.

None of these are capacity problems. Your business can do the work. The problem is information and preparation time.

TenderIQ is built specifically for this gap. AGPO filtered opportunities matched to your profile. Early alerts giving you real preparation time. Requirement breakdowns so you know exactly what to submit.

Registration was the door. TenderIQ is what gets you through it.

Waitlist at https://forms.gle/cCqPeLPxxoFZxY616

#AGPO #TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #Procurement

A question I get asked often: is government procurement in Kenya actually worth pursuing for a small business?

The honest answer is yes, with one condition.

Government contracts are attractive for SMEs for reasons most people do not consider. Payment is guaranteed by law once a contract is awarded and work is delivered. Contract values are often larger than what a typical private sector client offers. And a single government contract can provide the revenue stability that lets you invest in growing the rest of your business.

The condition: you have to be able to compete effectively. That means knowing about opportunities early, submitting complete and compliant bids, and doing this consistently not just once.

The businesses I have seen exit the cycle of inconsistent revenue and build something stable in Kenya are disproportionately the ones who cracked government procurement. Not because it is easy. Because it is reliable once you learn the system.

TenderIQ is built to make that system accessible to every qualifying SME, not just the ones with a procurement officer on staff or a consultant on retainer.

If you run a business in Kenya and want to make government contracts a serious revenue stream, this is what we are building for you.

Join the waitlist at https://forms.gle/cCqPeLPxxoFZxY616

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentContracts #Procurement #Kenya

The number one reason Kenyan SMEs lose government tenders they were qualified to win.

Incomplete documentation.

Not poor quality work. Not bad pricing. Incomplete documentation.

A bid gets disqualified before anyone reads it if a single required document is missing or expired. Tax compliance certificate. Business registration. AGPO certificate. CR12. Audited accounts. The list varies by tender and by procuring entity. And it is almost never the same twice.

Here is what makes this painful. Most SME owners only discover what was required after they lose. The feedback from a failed bid, if you even get it, often just says: disqualified, non responsive. No detail. No guidance.

So you submit again next time with the same gaps because you never learned what they were.

TenderIQ breaks down the requirements of every tender in plain language before you decide to bid. You see exactly what documents are needed, what qualifications are required and what evaluation criteria will be used to score the bid. You know before you start whether you are ready to compete, and if not, what you need to get in order.

No more being disqualified on technicalities you could have fixed with two days notice.

Waitlist at https://forms.gle/cCqPeLPxxoFZxY616

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #Procurement #AGPO

Something I noticed while building TenderIQ that I did not expect.

The Kenyan businesses winning government tenders consistently are not the ones with the best products or services. They are the ones with the best systems.

They have a person or a process that checks procurement portals regularly. They have a document folder that is always current so they can respond fast. They have a template for bid documents that gets adapted rather than rebuilt from scratch each time. They treat procurement like a pipeline, not a lottery.

The result looks like luck from the outside. It is not luck. It is a repeatable process that most small businesses cannot afford to build and maintain manually.

TenderIQ gives every qualifying Kenyan SME access to the same systematic approach that the consistently winning businesses use, without hiring a procurement officer or a consultant.

Automated monitoring across nine portals. Profile matched alerts. Requirement analysis before you commit time to a bid. Deadline tracking so preparation is never a scramble.

The system is the advantage. We are making it accessible.

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #Procurement #Kenya

I want to talk about WhatsApp procurement groups.

You probably know the ones. Someone adds you. Every day a flood of tender notices forwarded from someone who forwarded them from someone else. Half of them are already closed. A quarter are from three months ago. The rest you cannot tell because the image is too blurry to read the date.

This is currently the primary way many Kenyan SMEs discover government tenders. A crowdsourced, unverified, frequently outdated chain of forwards.

I am not criticising the people sharing. They are trying to help. I am pointing out that this is what passes for a procurement intelligence system for most small businesses in Kenya and it is genuinely not good enough.

You deserve better than a blurry screenshot forwarded at 11pm.

TenderIQ pulls directly from official procurement portals. Every tender is current, sourced and dated accurately. You see it when it is published, not when someone happens to forward it four days later.

Real information. Right time. Right business.

That is the difference between a WhatsApp group and a procurement intelligence platform.

Join the waitlist at https://forms.gle/cCqPeLPxxoFZxY616

Here is the first thing TenderIQ does when you sign up.

It asks you about your business. Sector. Services or goods you supply. Location. AGPO status. Business registration type.

That profile becomes your filter. From that point on every tender we pull from nine government procurement portals gets matched against your profile before it reaches you.

You do not see every tender in Kenya. You see every tender in Kenya that your business can realistically compete for.

This matters more than it sounds. The volume of government tenders published weekly in Kenya is significant. An unfiltered feed is noise. A profile matched feed is a pipeline.

The businesses currently winning tenders consistently have someone doing this matching manually. Reading through listings and deciding what is relevant. That takes hours. TenderIQ does it in seconds.

Your inbox becomes a list of genuine opportunities, not a research project.

We are in final development now. The waitlist at https://forms.gle/cCqPeLPxxoFZxY616 gets you first access when we launch.

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #Procurement #Kenya

The second thing TenderIQ does after matching a tender to your profile.

It reads the tender document so you do not have to start from scratch.

Government tender documents in Kenya range from four pages to over a hundred. They are written in procurement language that is precise but not always clear to someone running a business day to day. The requirements are buried in sections. The evaluation criteria are spread across different parts of the document.

TenderIQ's AI analyzer reads the full tender document and gives you a plain language breakdown.

What the procuring entity is buying. The exact documents you need to submit. The qualifications being evaluated and how they are scored. The financial requirements. The deadline and submission instructions.

You get this summary before you decide whether to bid. So the decision is informed, not a guess.

If you qualify and have everything required you move forward with confidence. If there is a gap you know about it before you have invested days in a bid you will not win.

This is the feature I am most proud of. It levels the information gap that has kept too many good businesses out of tenders they deserved to win.

Waitlist at https://forms.gle/cCqPeLPxxoFZxY616

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #Procurement #AGPO

Deadline tracking is not a small feature. Let me explain why.

A government tender in Kenya typically runs for two to four weeks from publication to closing date. Some are shorter. The preparation time required for a competitive bid, gathering documents, writing technical proposals, getting sign offs, varies by tender but is never zero.

If you find a tender with six days to closing and you need eight days of preparation time, you have already lost. You can submit but it will be rushed and incomplete bids rarely win.

The businesses that win consistently find tenders early. That is the whole game. Finding them early gives you the full preparation window. Finding them late means competing at a disadvantage even when you qualify.

TenderIQ alerts you when a matching tender is published, not when it is about to close. You get the full timeline. You can plan your preparation properly. You can decide rationally whether you have enough time to compete well rather than scrambling and hoping.

The difference between finding a tender on day one and finding it on day twelve of a two week window is often the difference between a winning bid and a disqualified one.

Early information is the advantage. That is what TenderIQ delivers.

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #Procurement #Kenya

The nine procurement portals TenderIQ monitors and why each one matters.

PPIP: The Public Procurement Information Portal. This is the primary national government procurement platform. If you only check one portal this is the one but one is not enough.

NCC: Nairobi City County. Kenya's capital city procurement. Significant contract volumes across services, construction and supplies.

KenGen: Kenya Electricity Generating Company. Energy sector procurement including equipment, services and infrastructure.

KeNHA: Kenya National Highways Authority. Roads and highways procurement. Large contracts and supply chain opportunities.

KeRRA: Kenya Rural Roads Authority. Rural infrastructure procurement, often with strong AGPO components.

KURA: Kenya Urban Roads Authority. Urban road network procurement in cities outside Nairobi.

KPC: Kenya Pipeline Company. Petroleum infrastructure procurement.

KAA: Kenya Airports Authority. Aviation sector procurement across all major airports.

GDC: Geothermal Development Company. Energy sector procurement with significant goods and services components.

Nine portals. Different login systems. Different update frequencies. Different formats.

TenderIQ monitors all of them continuously so you get one feed, matched to your business.

Waitlist at https://forms.gle/cCqPeLPxxoFZxY616

For an SME bidding on a KES 500,000 contract that fee might make sense. For one bidding on a KES 80,000 supply contract the economics collapse immediately.

So what happens? Most small businesses either pay fees that eat deeply into potential margins or they go it alone without the expertise and submit bids that get disqualified on technicalities.

Neither option is good. And neither option should be the only choice available.

TenderIQ is being built to sit in the gap. Not a replacement for a procurement professional on a complex high value bid. But a system that handles the information and preparation work that currently makes procurement inaccessible for smaller opportunities.

Knowing about the tender early. Understanding what is required. Tracking the deadline. Knowing which documents to gather before you start writing.

That is the work TenderIQ does. So you can do the work that actually requires your expertise, preparing and submitting a bid that reflects what your business can genuinely deliver.

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #Procurement #Kenya

Five documents every Kenyan SME should have current at all times if you are serious about government procurement.

First: KRA Tax Compliance Certificate. Required for virtually every government tender. It expires every twelve months. The number of bids that get disqualified because this certificate lapsed mid preparation is significant. Renew it before it expires, not after.

Second: Business Registration Certificate. Your certificate of incorporation or business name registration. Certified copies are often required. Know where your originals are.

Third: CR12 from the Companies Registry. For limited companies this confirms your directors and shareholding. Some tenders require it to be recent, within three or six months.

Fourth: AGPO Certificate if you are eligible. Youth, women or PWD owned. This is what unlocks the 30% set aside. If you qualify and you do not have this certificate you are leaving reserved procurement opportunities on the table.

Fifth: Audited Financial Accounts. Many tenders require the last one to three years of audited accounts. If yours are not up to date this will knock you out of larger value bids.

Get these in order now, before you find a tender you want to bid on. Scrambling to get compliance documents in the last 48 hours before a deadline is how otherwise strong bids fall apart.

TenderIQ will tell you exactly which of these each tender requires. But having them ready is on you.

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #AGPO #Procurement

How to read a government tender document in Kenya without spending three hours on it.

Most tender documents follow a similar structure even when they look different on the surface. Here is where to look first.

Section one: go straight to the scope of work or terms of reference. This tells you what the procuring entity actually wants. Read this before anything else. If your business cannot deliver what is described you can stop reading.

Section two: find the eligibility criteria. This is usually in the instructions to bidders. It lists who can bid. Check your AGPO status requirement, minimum years of operation, turnover thresholds if any, and sector specific licenses required.

Section three: find the mandatory documents checklist. This is often attached as an annex or listed in the bid data sheet. It is your submission checklist. Every item on it is a potential disqualification if missing.

Section four: find the evaluation criteria. This tells you how bids will be scored. Technical versus financial weighting. Specific experience requirements that will be evaluated. This tells you what to emphasize in your proposal.

Section five: closing date and submission instructions. Physical or online submission. Exact time. Location if physical. Late submissions are never accepted.

TenderIQ extracts all of this into a summary before you decide to bid. But knowing the structure helps you verify the analysis and move faster.

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #Procurement #Kenya

The difference between a compliant bid and a winning bid.

This is something most procurement advice gets wrong. There are two separate thresholds in government tender evaluation.

The first threshold is compliance. Your bid either meets the mandatory requirements or it does not. Missing documents, expired certificates, failure to follow submission instructions. If you fail compliance your bid is not evaluated. It is simply rejected.

Most SMEs focus their anxiety on this threshold. They should. But it is the floor, not the ceiling.

The second threshold is competitiveness. Among the bids that pass compliance, which one wins? This is where technical quality, relevant experience, methodology and pricing are evaluated and scored.

A lot of SMEs clear the compliance threshold and then submit technically weak proposals because they spent all their energy on documentation and ran out of time to write a strong technical bid.

The solution is not to work harder. It is to have more time. Finding the tender early. Knowing requirements in advance. Having your compliance documents ready before you start.

When documentation is not a crisis you can focus on writing the kind of technical proposal that actually scores well.

TenderIQ is designed to give you that time back. Information early enough that preparation is planned, not panicked.

Waitlist at https://forms.gle/cCqPeLPxxoFZxY616

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #Procurement #AGPO

Three things the most consistent tender winners in Kenya do that most SMEs do not.

First: they treat procurement as a pipeline, not an event. They are not looking for one big tender. They are building a consistent flow of bids submitted regularly across the year. Some win. Some do not. The volume makes the wins inevitable.

Second: they debrief every loss. When a bid is unsuccessful they request feedback from the procuring entity. They find out exactly why they lost. Score sheets when available. Evaluator notes when accessible. They use that information to improve the next submission.

Third: they maintain their compliance documents proactively. KRA certificate current. Business registration certified. AGPO certificate valid. Audited accounts filed. These are never urgent because they are never left to expire.

None of these practices require expensive consultants or inside connections. They require systems and discipline.

TenderIQ provides the system for the first one. Continuous, matched, early tender discovery so your pipeline stays full.

The discipline and the debrief habit are yours to build. But you cannot build a pipeline on information you never receive in time to act on.

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #Procurement #Kenya

What happens after you win your first government tender in Kenya.

I talk a lot about getting to the point of winning. But it is worth talking about what comes after because it changes how you prepare.

Once a tender is awarded and a contract is signed the procuring entity issues a local purchase order or a contract agreement depending on the value. You deliver. You submit an invoice. Payment is processed through the government payment systems.

Payment timelines vary. County governments and some state corporations have historically been slower than national government entities. The law provides for payment within a defined period but enforcement is imperfect.

What this means practically: you need working capital to deliver before payment arrives. Many SMEs win their first government contract and then struggle to deliver because they cannot finance the gap between delivery and payment.

Plan for this before you win. Not after.

Know your delivery cost. Know your float. Know which bank or SACCO you can approach for a short term facility against a signed government contract, which most lenders will consider favorably.

The tender is the beginning. Financial readiness determines whether winning it becomes a success or a strain.

TenderIQ helps you find and win. Your financial preparation helps you deliver.

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #Procurement #AGPO

One month ago I started sharing what I was building and why.

I did not know how these posts would land. Procurement is not a glamorous topic. There is no viral moment in a post about KRA compliance certificates.

What I found is that the Kenyan SME community understands this problem deeply and personally. The responses I have received, in comments and in DMs, are from real business owners who have felt every frustration I have been describing.

Missed deadlines. Blurry WhatsApp forwards. Bids disqualified for missing a single document. Tenders found on day eleven of a fourteen day window.

This is the problem TenderIQ solves. And it matters more than I initially understood because government procurement is not a side opportunity for Kenyan SMEs. For many it is the path to the kind of contract value and payment reliability that changes what a small business can become.

We are launching soon. The waitlist is how we are managing early access.

If you have been following these posts and have not yet signed up, now is the time. The form is at https://forms.gle/cCqPeLPxxoFZxY616 and it takes two minutes.

Thank you for following along. The best part is coming.

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #Procurement #Kenya

Tomorrow I am announcing the TenderIQ launch date.

Before I do I want to say something to every SME owner who has been following these posts.

You are not behind. You are not too small. You are not too late.

The government procurement system in Kenya was not designed with you in mind. It was designed for procurement officers and large businesses that could afford to dedicate resources to monitoring it. AGPO was a correction but information access was never fully fixed.

TenderIQ is the rest of that correction.

If your business qualifies, whether through AGPO or open tenders, you deserve access to the same opportunities that have been visible to better resourced competitors all along.

That access is almost ready.

Get on the waitlist tonight if you have not already. Tomorrow is the announcement and waitlist members get first access before the public launch.

Form is at https://forms.gle/cCqPeLPxxoFZxY616

See you tomorrow.

#TenderIQ #KenyanSMEs #GovernmentTenders #Procurement #Kenya

TenderIQ is launching.

Thirty days ago I shared why I was building this. A procurement system that was failing Kenyan SMEs not through bad intentions but through poor design. Information scattered. Opportunities invisible. Deadlines arriving before preparation could begin.

Today that changes.

TenderIQ is now open for early access to waitlist members. Here is what you get from day one.

A profile matched tender feed pulling from nine government procurement portals updated continuously. Alerts when a relevant tender is published with enough time to prepare properly. AI powered requirement analysis breaking down every tender document into plain language before you decide to bid. Deadline tracking so nothing slips.

Mpesa payments supported. KES pricing built for SME budgets.

If you are on the waitlist you will receive your access link separately today.

If you are seeing this and have not yet signed up, the waitlist is still open and we are onboarding in batches. Sign up now at https://forms.gle/cCqPeLPxxoFZxY616 and you will be in the next access group.

Thank you to everyone who followed this journey, commented, shared and sent messages of encouragement over the past month. You validated that this needed to be built.

Now go find your next tender.
